introduction: This spec defines the foundational backend infrastructure for af-hub — the coordination hub of the agent-fox platform — covering project layout, TOML configuration loading, SQLite schema initialization, store layer CRUD operations, admin bootstrap and token rotation, health probes, structured JSON logging, and graceful shutdown. Note: requirements referencing external library APIs (github.com/labstack/echo/v4, github.com/BurntSushi/toml, modernc.org/sqlite, github.com/sirupsen/logrus, github.com/google/uuid) are based on PRD descriptions without a Verified External API section and should be cross-checked against installed library versions before implementation.

glossary.--reset-admin-token: Server startup flag that triggers admin token rotation, generating and persisting a new admin token.
glossary./healthz: The liveness probe HTTP endpoint that always returns 200 if the server process is alive.
glossary./readyz: The readiness probe HTTP endpoint that checks database connectivity before returning 200.
glossary.AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN: Environment variable holding the plaintext admin token, required on all non-first boots to validate the admin credential.
glossary.WAL mode: SQLite Write-Ahead Logging mode, enabled on database open for concurrent read/write safety.
glossary.admin bootstrap: The automatic process on first boot that creates the admin user, generates an admin token, and writes it to disk.
glossary.admin_token: The plaintext file (mode 0600) written next to config.toml containing the generated admin token.
glossary.admin_tokens: SQLite table that stores the SHA-256 hash of the current admin token.
glossary.af-hub: The server binary that acts as the coordination hub for the agent-fox platform.
glossary.afc: The CLI client binary for the agent-fox platform, stubbed in this spec.
glossary.api_keys: SQLite table storing API key records for users and workspaces.
glossary.config.toml: The TOML configuration file that the server loads from the current working directory on startup.
glossary.drain timeout: The 15-second window after SIGTERM/SIGINT during which the server waits for in-flight HTTP connections to complete before force-closing.
glossary.first boot: The initial server startup when zero user records exist in the `users` table, triggering admin bootstrap.
glossary.key_hash: The stored hash of an API key in the `api_keys` table.
glossary.key_id: The public identifier portion of an API key stored in the `api_keys` table.
glossary.provider: The authentication provider name (e.g. 'local', 'github') stored on a user record.
glossary.provider_id: The provider-scoped unique identifier for a user, stored alongside `provider`.
glossary.slug: A URL-safe lowercase identifier derived from a workspace name, stored in the `workspaces` table.
glossary.token_hash: The SHA-256 hash of the plaintext admin token stored in the `admin_tokens` table.
glossary.users: SQLite table storing all user records including the bootstrap admin user.
glossary.workspace_members: SQLite table storing membership associations between users and workspaces.
glossary.CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS: SQL statement to create a table only if it does not already exist.
glossary.PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL: SQLite pragma enabling Write-Ahead Logging for concurrency.
glossary.README.md: Project root documentation file with quickstart guide.
glossary.[[auth.oauth]]: TOML array-of-tables section for OAuth provider configuration.
glossary.[database] path: TOML config field: filesystem path to the SQLite database.
glossary.[logging] level: TOML config field: minimum log level for output.
glossary.[server] bind_address: TOML config field: network address the server binds to.
glossary.[server] external_url: TOML config field: public-facing URL for OAuth redirect derivation.
glossary.[server] port: TOML config field: TCP port the server listens on.
glossary.af_admin_: Token prefix identifying an admin bootstrap token.
glossary.bin/: Output directory for compiled Go binaries.
glossary.bin/af-hub: Compiled af-hub server binary output path.
glossary.bin/afc: Compiled afc CLI binary output path.
glossary.cmd/af-hub/: Go entry point directory for the af-hub server binary.
glossary.cmd/afc/: Go entry point directory for the afc CLI binary.
glossary.crypto/rand: Go stdlib package for cryptographically secure random number generation.
glossary.github.com/BurntSushi/toml: Go library for TOML configuration file parsing.
glossary.github.com/sirupsen/logrus: Go library for structured JSON logging.
Go command to run all tests recursively.
Go command to run static analysis checks.
glossary.internal/: Go private package directory; not importable outside the module.
glossary.make build: Makefile target that compiles both Go binaries.
glossary.make lint: Makefile target that runs go vet.
glossary.make test: Makefile target that runs all tests.
glossary.os.MkdirAll: Go stdlib function to create a directory path recursively.
glossary.time.Parse(time.RFC3339, v): Go expression to parse an RFC 3339 timestamp string.
glossary.username: Unique string identifier for a user account.
glossary.warn: Log level for important but non-critical messages.
glossary.{\"status\": \"not ready\"}: JSON response body when the readiness probe fails.
glossary.{\"status\": \"ok\"}: JSON response body for a successful liveness probe.
glossary.{\"status\": \"ready\"}: JSON response body for a successful readiness probe.
glossary.modernc.org/sqlite: Pure-Go SQLite driver; no CGo dependency.
glossary.(provider, provider_id): Composite unique constraint on user records.
glossary.name: Display name field for a workspace.
glossary.(user_id, workspace_id): Composite primary key for workspace membership.
glossary.url: Workspace URL field storing the external reference.
glossary.time.RFC3339: Go time format constant for RFC 3339 timestamps.
glossary.status: User account status field (active or blocked).

requirements.title: Go project layout and build targets

user_story.goal: build both server and CLI binaries with a single make command
user_story.benefit: I can reliably produce deployable artifacts and run tests from the repository root

acceptance_criteria.action: contains entry points at `cmd/af-hub/` for the server binary and `cmd/afc/` for the CLI binary, with all private packages under `internal/`
compiles both `af-hub` and `afc` binaries and places them at `bin/af-hub` and `bin/afc` respectively
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits with code 0 on success; exits with non-zero on compilation error

acceptance_criteria.action: executes `go test ./...` across all packages
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits with code 0 when all tests pass; exits with non-zero on any test failure

acceptance_criteria.action: executes `go vet ./...` across all packages
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits with code 0 when no vet errors are found; exits with non-zero otherwise

acceptance_criteria.action: is a valid Go binary stub that compiles and exits cleanly, deferring all CLI functionality to a later spec

edge_cases.action: emits compiler error output to stderr and exits with a non-zero code without producing partial binaries in `bin/`
edge_cases.error_condition: a source file contains a compilation error

user_story.goal: configure the server via a TOML file with validated fields and sensible defaults
user_story.benefit: I can tune server behavior without silent misconfiguration causing runtime failures

acceptance_criteria.action: loads `config.toml` from the current working directory using `github.com/BurntSushi/toml` and applies defaults: `[server] port` = 8080, `[server] bind_address` = "0.0.0.0", `[database] path` = "./data/af-hub.db", `[logging] level` = "info"

validates that `[server] port` is an integer in the range 1–65535, `[logging] level` is one of trace/debug/info/warn/error/fatal/panic, and all required fields in each `[[auth.oauth]]` entry (`provider`, `client_id`, `client_secret`) are non-empty strings
creates the directory tree automatically with `os.MkdirAll` before opening the database
acceptance_criteria.trigger: when the parent directory of the configured `[database] path` does not exist

acceptance_criteria.action: makes the value available to other subsystems for OAuth redirect URI derivation; no validation is applied beyond being a non-empty string when present

edge_cases.action: logs a fatal error message and refuses to start
edge_cases.return_contract: exits with non-zero code before binding any port
edge_cases.error_condition: `config.toml` is absent from the current working directory

edge_cases.action: logs a descriptive fatal error identifying the invalid field and refuses to start
edge_cases.return_contract: exits with non-zero code before binding any port
edge_cases.error_condition: `config.toml` contains an invalid value (e.g. port out of range, unknown log level, missing required OAuth field)

edge_cases.action: logs a fatal error with the underlying OS error and refuses to start
edge_cases.error_condition: the data directory cannot be created due to a filesystem permission error

requirements.title: SQLite database initialization with WAL mode

user_story.goal: have the database schema created automatically on server startup
user_story.benefit: I can deploy the server without running separate migration commands

acceptance_criteria.action: enables WAL mode by executing `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL` immediately after opening the connection using the pure-Go `modernc.org/sqlite` driver
acceptance_criteria.trigger: when it opens the SQLite database

acceptance_criteria.action: executes `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` statements for all five tables — `users`, `workspaces`, `workspace_members`, `api_keys`, and `admin_tokens` — with the exact column definitions and constraints specified in the PRD

enforces a UNIQUE constraint on `(provider, provider_id)` in the `users` table, a composite primary key `(user_id, workspace_id)` in `workspace_members`, and UNIQUE constraints on `name`, `slug`, and `url` in `workspaces`
stores all timestamps as RFC 3339 TEXT strings and all primary keys as UUID TEXT values

edge_cases.action: logs a fatal error with the underlying OS error and refuses to start; no partial state is left open
edge_cases.error_condition: the SQLite database file cannot be opened or created (e.g. directory not writable)

edge_cases.action: logs a fatal error identifying the failing table and refuses to start; closes the database connection before exiting
edge_cases.error_condition: a `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` statement fails (e.g. schema conflict with existing incompatible table)

user_story.goal: interact with all database entities through typed Go functions
user_story.benefit: I can build higher-level features without writing raw SQL outside the store layer

acceptance_criteria.action: provides typed Go functions for create, read (by ID and by relevant unique fields), update, and delete operations on each of the five tables: `users`, `workspaces`, `workspace_members`, `api_keys`, and `admin_tokens`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: each function returns a typed value and an error (Go `(*T, error)` pattern); returns a sentinel not-found error when a record does not exist

acceptance_criteria.action: generates all new entity IDs as UUIDs using `github.com/google/uuid` and formats all timestamps as RFC 3339 strings using `time.RFC3339` before persisting
is the only package in the codebase that executes SQL statements directly against the database; all other packages interact with data exclusively through store layer function calls
wraps all database errors with contextual information (operation name and entity type) before returning them to callers
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns a non-nil error value; never panics or calls os.Exit

edge_cases.action: returns a typed constraint-violation error that callers can distinguish from other database errors
edge_cases.return_contract: returns a non-nil error; does not panic
edge_cases.error_condition: a create operation violates a UNIQUE constraint (e.g. duplicate `username` in `users`)

edge_cases.action: returns a wrapped error to the caller with the operation context; does not retry internally; does not call os.Exit
edge_cases.error_condition: a database operation times out or the connection is lost mid-query

user_story.goal: have an admin user and token created automatically on the first server start
user_story.benefit: I can access the system immediately after deployment without manual credential setup

acceptance_criteria.action: creates an admin user record with `username` = "admin", `email` = "admin@localhost", `provider` = "local", `provider_id` = "admin", and `status` = "active" in the `users` table via the store layer
acceptance_criteria.condition: zero records exist in the `users` table

acceptance_criteria.action: generates a cryptographically random 32-byte token using `crypto/rand`, hex-encodes it to produce a 64-character string, and prepends `af_admin_` to form the full admin token
acceptance_criteria.trigger: after the admin user record is created
acceptance_criteria.condition: admin bootstrap is in progress (first boot detected)

acceptance_criteria.action: computes the SHA-256 hash of the plaintext token and inserts a record into `admin_tokens` with the resulting `token_hash` via the store layer
acceptance_criteria.trigger: after the plaintext admin token is generated

acceptance_criteria.action: writes the plaintext admin token to a file named `admin_token` in the same directory as `config.toml`, with file permissions 0600
acceptance_criteria.trigger: after the token hash is persisted

acceptance_criteria.action: logs the absolute path of the `admin_token` file at the `warn` log level

edge_cases.action: logs a fatal error, removes any partially written file, and refuses to start; the admin user record created in this bootstrap attempt is also removed to leave the database in a clean state
edge_cases.error_condition: the `admin_token` file cannot be written during admin bootstrap (e.g. directory not writable)

edge_cases.action: returns an error to the startup routine, which logs it as fatal and refuses to start; no partial token or file is left on disk
edge_cases.error_condition: crypto/rand fails to generate random bytes during admin token generation

requirements.title: Admin token validation on subsequent boots

user_story.goal: have the server validate the admin token from the environment on every non-first startup
user_story.benefit: accidental or unauthorized starts without the correct token are rejected before the server accepts traffic

acceptance_criteria.action: reads the `AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN` environment variable, computes its SHA-256 hash, and compares it against the `token_hash` stored in the `admin_tokens` table
acceptance_criteria.condition: at least one record exists in the `users` table (not first boot)

acceptance_criteria.action: logs a fatal error indicating the missing environment variable and refuses to start
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits with non-zero code before binding any port
acceptance_criteria.trigger: when the server performs admin token validation on a non-first boot

acceptance_criteria.action: logs a fatal error indicating token mismatch and refuses to start
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits with non-zero code before binding any port
acceptance_criteria.trigger: when the server performs admin token validation on a non-first boot
acceptance_criteria.condition: `AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN` is present but its SHA-256 hash does not match the stored `token_hash`

edge_cases.action: logs a fatal error indicating a corrupted token state and refuses to start
edge_cases.error_condition: the `admin_tokens` table is empty on a non-first boot (users exist but no token record)

user_story.goal: rotate the admin token by passing a flag at server startup
user_story.benefit: I can invalidate a compromised or lost admin token without manual database intervention

acceptance_criteria.action: generates a new cryptographically random admin token using the same algorithm as admin bootstrap, updates the `admin_tokens` table with the new `token_hash`, overwrites the `admin_token` file (mode 0600), logs the file path at warn level, then continues normal startup
skips the `AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN` environment variable validation step that would otherwise occur on a non-first boot
acceptance_criteria.trigger: after the new token is persisted

edge_cases.action: logs a fatal error, does not update the `admin_tokens` table (or rolls back any update already made), and refuses to start, leaving the old token intact
edge_cases.error_condition: the `admin_token` file cannot be overwritten during token rotation

user_story.goal: query liveness and readiness endpoints on the running server
user_story.benefit: the orchestrator can automatically restart unhealthy pods and route traffic only to ready instances

acceptance_criteria.action: responds with HTTP 200 and JSON body `{"status": "ok"}` unconditionally, without touching the database
responds with HTTP 200 and JSON body `{"status": "ready"}`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: when it receives `GET /readyz` and the database ping succeeds

acceptance_criteria.action: responds with HTTP 503 and JSON body `{"status": "not ready"}`

edge_cases.action: applies a context deadline (maximum 2 seconds) to the ping operation; if the deadline is exceeded, returns HTTP 503 with body `{"status": "not ready"}` and logs the timeout at warn level
edge_cases.error_condition: the database ping for `/readyz` hangs indefinitely

user_story.goal: have every HTTP request logged as a structured JSON line
user_story.benefit: I can query and aggregate request logs in log aggregation systems without custom parsers

acceptance_criteria.action: uses `github.com/sirupsen/logrus` configured in JSON formatter mode as the sole logging backend
emits a structured JSON log entry containing at minimum: HTTP method, request path, response status code, and request duration in milliseconds
sets the logrus global log level to the configured value before the HTTP server begins accepting connections
acceptance_criteria.trigger: when the log level configured in `config.toml` is applied

user_story.goal: have the server drain in-flight requests before exiting on a stop signal
user_story.benefit: deployments and restarts do not abruptly terminate active connections

acceptance_criteria.action: stops accepting new connections and initiates graceful shutdown with a `drain timeout` of 15 seconds using the Echo framework's Shutdown method
acceptance_criteria.trigger: when it receives SIGTERM or SIGINT

acceptance_criteria.action: closes the database connection, flushes log buffers, and exits with code 0
acceptance_criteria.condition: all in-flight connections drain within the `drain timeout`

acceptance_criteria.action: force-closes all remaining connections, closes the database connection, logs a warning indicating forced shutdown, and exits
acceptance_criteria.condition: in-flight connections do not drain within the `drain timeout`

edge_cases.action: applies a maximum 5-second timeout to the database close operation; if exceeded, logs the error and exits without blocking indefinitely
edge_cases.error_condition: the database connection close call hangs after the drain phase completes

user_story.goal: find clear documentation for the project structure, configuration, and architecture
user_story.benefit: I can get the server running and understand its design without reading source code

acceptance_criteria.action: contains `README.md` at the project root covering: project overview, prerequisites (Go 1.22+), quickstart steps (build, configure, run, first boot), project structure overview, and links to `docs/architecture.md` and `docs/configuration.md`
contains `docs/architecture.md` covering: two-binary design (`af-hub` and `afc`), `internal/` package layout, SQLite storage rationale, config loading flow, and request lifecycle
contains `docs/configuration.md` with a complete `config.toml` reference listing every field, its type, default value, validation rules, and any environment variable override

correctness_properties.title: Admin token hash consistency
correctness_properties.for_any: plaintext admin token written to the `admin_token` file
correctness_properties.invariant: the SHA-256 hash of that token equals the `token_hash` stored in the `admin_tokens` table at the time of generation or rotation

correctness_properties.title: Admin bootstrap exactly once
correctness_properties.invariant: admin bootstrap (user creation, token generation, file write) executes if and only if zero records exist in the `users` table at the time of the check; it never executes more than once per first-boot event

correctness_properties.title: Store layer exclusivity
correctness_properties.for_any: SQL statement executed against the database
correctness_properties.invariant: the statement originates from within the store layer package; no other package executes SQL directly

correctness_properties.title: RFC 3339 timestamp consistency
correctness_properties.for_any: timestamp value written to any table column
correctness_properties.invariant: the value is a valid RFC 3339 string parseable by `time.Parse(time.RFC3339, v)`

correctness_properties.title: No port binding before successful validation
correctness_properties.for_any: server startup sequence that fails configuration, schema, or token validation
correctness_properties.invariant: the server never binds to a TCP port before all startup validations pass

correctness_properties.title: WAL mode always enabled before schema initialization
correctness_properties.invariant: `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL` is executed before any `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` statement

execution_paths.title: First boot: admin bootstrap and server ready

steps.action: Invokes `bin/af-hub` with `config.toml` present in the working directory and no `AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN` set
Reads and validates `config.toml`; applies defaults for missing optional fields
Opens the SQLite file at the configured `[database] path`, enables WAL mode, and executes all `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` statements
Detects zero records in `users` table, creates the admin user record via the store layer
Generates a 64-hex-char random token prefixed with `af_admin_`, stores its SHA-256 `token_hash` in `admin_tokens` via the store layer
Writes the plaintext token to the `admin_token` file (mode 0600) next to `config.toml` and logs the file path at warn level
Binds to the configured address and port; registers `/healthz` and `/readyz` routes with request logging middleware
Observes the server accepting connections and reads the plaintext token from the `admin_token` file

execution_paths.title: Subsequent boot: admin token validation and server ready

steps.action: Sets `AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN` to the plaintext token from the `admin_token` file and invokes `bin/af-hub`
Reads and validates `config.toml`
Opens SQLite, enables WAL mode, runs `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` (no-ops on existing tables)
Reads `AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN`, computes its SHA-256 hash, fetches `token_hash` from `admin_tokens`, and confirms they match
Binds to the configured address and port and begins accepting requests
Invokes `bin/af-hub --reset-admin-token`
Reads and validates `config.toml`
Opens SQLite, enables WAL mode, runs `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`
Generates a new random admin token, updates `admin_tokens` with the new `token_hash`, overwrites the `admin_token` file (mode 0600), and logs the file path at warn level
Skips environment token validation and starts normally, binding to the configured address
Sends SIGTERM to the running `af-hub` process
Catches SIGTERM and triggers the Echo Shutdown method with a 15-second context deadline
Stops accepting new connections and waits for in-flight requests to complete
On drain completion (or timeout expiry), closes the database connection and exits
Sends `GET /readyz` to the server
Attempts a database ping with a 2-second context deadline
Ping times out or returns an error; responds with HTTP 503 and body `{"status": "not ready"}`
Marks the pod as not ready and stops routing traffic to it

error_handling.condition: `config.toml` is missing from the working directory at startup
error_handling.behavior: Server logs a fatal error and exits with non-zero code before binding any port

error_handling.condition: `config.toml` contains an invalid field value (port out of range, unknown log level, missing required OAuth field)
error_handling.behavior: Server logs a descriptive fatal error identifying the invalid field and exits with non-zero code before binding any port

error_handling.condition: Data directory cannot be created due to filesystem permission error
error_handling.behavior: Server logs a fatal error with the OS error detail and exits with non-zero code

error_handling.condition: SQLite database file cannot be opened or created
error_handling.behavior: Server logs a fatal error with the OS error, ensures no connection is left open, and exits with non-zero code

error_handling.condition: A `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` statement fails due to a schema conflict
error_handling.behavior: Server logs a fatal error identifying the failing table, closes the database connection, and exits with non-zero code

error_handling.condition: Store layer create operation violates a UNIQUE constraint
error_handling.behavior: Store layer returns a typed constraint-violation error to the caller; does not panic

error_handling.condition: Database operation times out or connection is lost mid-query in the store layer
error_handling.behavior: Store layer returns a wrapped error with operation context to the caller; does not retry internally; does not call os.Exit

error_handling.condition: `admin_token` file cannot be written during admin bootstrap
error_handling.behavior: Server removes any partially written file, removes the admin user record created in this bootstrap attempt, logs a fatal error, and exits with non-zero code

error_handling.condition: crypto/rand fails to generate random bytes during admin token generation
error_handling.behavior: Server propagates the error to the startup routine, which logs it as fatal and exits without leaving partial token state on disk

error_handling.condition: `AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN` is absent or empty on a non-first boot
error_handling.behavior: Server logs a fatal error indicating the missing variable and exits with non-zero code before binding any port

error_handling.condition: `AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN` hash does not match the stored `token_hash` on a non-first boot
error_handling.behavior: Server logs a fatal error indicating token mismatch and exits with non-zero code before binding any port

error_handling.condition: `admin_tokens` table is empty on a non-first boot
error_handling.behavior: Server logs a fatal error indicating corrupted token state and exits with non-zero code

error_handling.condition: `admin_token` file cannot be overwritten during `--reset-admin-token` rotation
error_handling.behavior: Server does not update `admin_tokens` table (or rolls back any update), logs a fatal error, leaves the old token intact, and exits with non-zero code
Handler returns HTTP 503 with body `{"status": "not ready"}` and logs the timeout at warn level

error_handling.condition: Database connection close hangs after the graceful shutdown drain phase
error_handling.behavior: Server applies a 5-second timeout to the close operation; if exceeded, logs the error and exits without blocking indefinitely

symbols.name: New
symbols.notes: Creates a new Echo instance. Verify exact signature against installed version.

symbols.name: Echo.Shutdown
symbols.notes: Gracefully shuts down the server without interrupting active connections. Used for 15-second drain.

symbols.name: DecodeFile
symbols.signature: func DecodeFile(fpath string, v interface{}) (MetaData, error)
symbols.notes: Decodes a TOML file into v. Verify exact signature against installed version.

symbols.name: Driver registration
symbols.notes: Pure-Go SQLite driver; registers the 'sqlite' driver name for use with database/sql. No CGo required.

symbols.name: SetFormatter
symbols.notes: Sets the global logrus formatter. Use with &logrus.JSONFormatter{} for structured JSON output.

symbols.name: SetLevel
symbols.notes: Sets the global logrus log level.

symbols.name: New
symbols.notes: Generates a new random UUID (v4). Returns UUID type; call .String() for the text representation.
